question: Why do frontier models need high-quality human feedback data?
answer_points: High-quality human feedback is hard to produce at scale: it requires annotators with real LLM expertise, quality-control infrastructure, and tooling that generic crowdsourcing platforms can't deliver.
Models and the real world keep changing, so static labeling instructions can't cover every edge case — fresh, ongoing human data is needed rather than a one-off dataset.

question: why do the rules for tagging data stop working over time?
answer_points: The models and the phenomena being labeled keep changing — what counts as spam/toxic today may not tomorrow, so yesterday's labeling rules go stale.
Static labeling instructions can never cover every edge case; new situations keep emerging that the original rules didn't anticipate, so the rules stop working over time.
